title: Ritvo Autism Asperger Diagnostic Scale-Revised (RAADS-R)
description: An 80-item self-report scale designed to identify adults (18+) with autism spectrum disorder who may have been missed in earlier assessments. Based on Ritvo et al., 2011.

scoring.scale: 0-3 per item (Never true=0, True only when young=1, True only now=2, True now and when young=3)

severity_levels.label: mild
severity_levels.description: Some ASD traits present — monitoring recommended

severity_levels.label: moderate
severity_levels.description: Significant ASD traits — clinical evaluation recommended

severity_levels.label: clinical
severity_levels.description: Strong indication of ASD — comprehensive assessment strongly recommended

questions.text: I am a sympathetic person
I often use words and phrases from movies and television in conversations
I am often surprised when I find out that what I said was rude or inappropriate
Sometimes I talk too loudly or too softly, and I am not aware of it
I often don't know how to act in social situations
I can 'chat' and make small talk with people
When I feel overwhelmed by my senses, I have to isolate myself to shut them down
How to make friends and socialize is a mystery to me
When talking to someone, I have a hard time telling when it is my turn to talk or listen
Sometimes I have to cover my ears to block out painful noises (like vacuum cleaners or people talking too much or too loudly)
It can be very hard to read someone's face, hand, and body movements when they are talking
I focus on details rather than the overall idea
I take things too literally, so I often miss what people are trying to say
I get extremely upset when the way I like to do things is suddenly changed
I want to be with other people
I need to try to figure out what other people's facial expressions mean
I may not know how someone is feeling even if they tell me
I like to copy the way certain people do things
I can determine if someone is upset or angry by their face
Certain daily textures that touch my skin (such as clothing tags) are bothersome
I don't like being hugged or held
I can suddenly become very angry when something doesn't go my way
Sometimes I don't understand why someone got offended by what I said
People say that I give them too much detail about things
I find it difficult to tell a lie, even a small white lie
I notice patterns in things all the time
I don't understand why some things upset people so much
I tend to point out others' mistakes
I was considered strange or weird as a child by my peers
I find it very difficult to play 'pretend' games with children
I sometimes have trouble keeping my body still
I have been told that I have an unusual voice (e.g., flat, monotone, childish, high-pitched)
I feel very comfortable with dating or being in social situations with others
I fixate on things that other people don't find interesting
I have extremely strong interests that I get upset about if I can't pursue them
I have repetitive routines that I follow and get upset if they are interrupted
I have always had difficulty with small talk and chit-chat
People have told me that I am clumsy
Certain sounds, touches, textures, and smells are overwhelming to me
I have been told by others that I seem 'different'
I understand and use sarcasm and humor in conversation
I get confused in large crowds of people
I am fascinated with numbers, dates, and facts
I often hum, sing, or make sounds to myself
I do not like changes in routine — getting used to changes takes a lot of effort
I find it hard to imagine what it would be like to be someone else
When I am interested in something, I collect as much information as possible
I often feel confused or left out in social gatherings
I miss cues that tell me a conversation topic has changed
As a child, I was bullied or picked on by others
I have the need to complete a particular activity before starting another
People sometimes say I have trouble understanding others' emotions
Sometimes I rock back and forth or side to side
I have trouble with coordination and balance
I can go for days eating the same food over and over
My voice has been described as flat or monotone by others
Bright lights, colors, and loud noises are uncomfortable to me
I have been described as having poor handwriting or being clumsy
I tend to organize things in a very specific way
I sometimes repeat certain words, phrases, or movements
I have difficulty with gross-motor skills (running, catching balls)
I flap my hands, twist objects, or have specific physical habits
I am very aware of certain textures or the feel of things against my skin
I have a strong preference for certain types of food textures
I may respond to noise or lights differently than most people
I find certain physical sensations very pleasurable (e.g., spinning)
I have strong negative reactions to certain smells or tastes
I have a tendency to walk on my toes or have an unusual gait
I seem less sensitive to pain than other people
Noisy or chaotic environments are very stressful for me
I find it difficult to tolerate specific lighting (fluorescent lights)
I sometimes cover my eyes to avoid bright lights
I notice small sounds that others do not seem to hear
I find certain movements or physical actions soothing (e.g., rocking)
I am very sensitive to temperature changes
I have strong physical reactions to being touched unexpectedly
I need more personal space than most people
I find it hard to use tools or utensils that require fine motor skills
I have trouble judging distances when driving or walking
I have unusual reactions to the way things feel, look, smell, taste, or sound

response_options.text: True only when I was younger than 16
True now and when I was young
